step_by_step_reasoning: We identify two user roles at the top: a hospital client sending medical images, and a pathologist who annotates these images. The images are stored in an input S3 bucket. An EKS cluster hosts the labelling and inference platform. The annotated data is used by SageMaker for training, leveraging FSx for Lustre and IAM for high-performance security. Once trained, the model is exported to an output S3 bucket, from which EKS loads the model for validation and inference.

graph.name: Kinaï: Scaling Medical AI Training and Inference on AWS
graph.notes: Medical technology platform scaling image analysis. Achieved 30x faster training and 30% cost reduction by moving from static infrastructure to dynamic SageMaker pipelines.

nodes.name: S3 Input Bucket
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores raw medical images, up to 5TB per project.

nodes.name: EKS Platform
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Runs image labelling tools and performs model inference.

nodes.name: SageMaker
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Runs containerized ML training pipelines.

nodes.name: FSx for Lustre
nodes.notes: Provides high-performance scratch space for SageMaker training.

nodes.name: IAM
nodes.notes: Enforces security compliance and data access control.

nodes.name: S3 Model Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores completed, trained machine learning models.

edges.notes: Raw images are loaded into EKS labelling interface.
Pathologist labels and annotates the images.
Enriched annotations are saved back to S3.
SageMaker loads annotated dataset for training.
SageMaker uses FSx for fast data throughput.
IAM manages secure access bounds during training.
Trained models are exported to S3.
EKS loads the trained model for validation and inference.
